scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from the date of accrual of the cause of action. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant, being acquainted with the Plaintiff, requested financial assistance of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) for his personal requirements. Acting on such request and reposing trust in the Defendant, the Plaintiff advanced the said sum on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer, bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456, as evidenced by Annexure A.

6. The Plaintiff further states that though there was no written agreement executed between the parties, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said loan amount and his liability to repay the same via WhatsApp messages dated {{WHATSAPP_MESSAGE_DATE}}, copies of which are produced as Annexure B. The loan was advanced on oral terms including repayment by 20.06.2024.

7. The Plaintiff states that despite the maturity of the loan and repeated oral demands for repayment, the Defendant failed to refund the amount. Consequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the Defendant, copy at Annexure C. The Defendant received the said notice but {{LEGAL_NOTICE_REPLY_DETAILS}}.

BREACH OF CONTRACT

8. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant was liable to repay the principal amount on demand or by 20.06.2024. The cause of action for recovery arose when the Defendant failed to repay the amount by the date of default agreed upon, i.e., 20.06.2024.

9. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant has committed a breach of the oral contract and trust reposed in him by withholding the lawful money of the Plaintiff despite the expiry of the agreed period and receipt of the legal notice demanding repayment.

DAMAGES

10. The Plaintiff states that due to the wrongful withholding of the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000/-, the Plaintiff has suffered financial loss. The Plaintiff is entitled to interest at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of default, i.e., 20.06.2024, till the date of realisation.

11. The Plaintiff further states that the Defendant's failure to repay has caused mental agony and harassment to the Plaintiff, necessitating the present litigation. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover the costs of this suit from the Defendant.

PROCEDURAL COMPLIANCE

12. The Plaintiff states that the provisions of Section 12A of the Commercial Courts, Commercial Division and Commercial Appellate Division of High Courts Act, 2015 have been complied with / are not applicable to the present suit as the dispute is between individuals for personal loan.

LEGAL BASIS

13. The present cause is governed by Indian Contract Act, 1872, including Section 73.

14. The present cause is governed by Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, including Section 9, Section 20, Section 34.

CAUSE OF ACTION

15. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

16. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 01.07.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

17. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

18. The suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Karnataka Co
